summary: This bundle records deterministic proof that the shipped Cautilus-owned readable artifact families listed in the projection matrix are generated from machine-readable packets or source artifacts and preserve packet-first truth boundaries.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: shipped Cautilus-owned readable views listed in docs/contracts/reviewable-artifact-projections.json
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: The satisfied claims cover shipped Cautilus-owned readable views listed in docs/contracts/reviewable-artifact-projections.json. JSON packets or declared source artifacts remain the audit source for these surfaces; Markdown and HTML are readable projections or review shells over those sources.
notClaimed: Every future Cautilus renderer family is covered automatically without being added to the projection matrix
Readable views prove behavior by themselves
HTML views are the source of truth
Every possible packet field is shown in every readable view
Consumer-owned custom report views follow this contract without their own tests

protectedBehaviors: projection matrix lists every shipped Cautilus-owned readable artifact family covered by this claim
each matrix row names source artifacts, source schemas, renderer path, command, tests, and proof markers
projection matrix is checked against the CLI command registry so shipped CLI readable renderers cannot silently disappear from the inventory
proof markers point at executable renderer tests and source-of-truth notices
claim discovery review Markdown is generated from claim/status packets and states that JSON remains the audit source
review prompt Markdown is generated from review prompt input packets by the portable prompt renderer tests
claim status Markdown and browser review preserve JSON packet audit-source and separate comment-packet boundaries
review render-prompt converts a prepared review prompt input packet into review.prompt.md through the CLI
review prompt output keeps the meta-prompt seam explicit instead of treating Markdown as source state
report, review, review-summary, compare, scenario proposal, conversation review, evidence bundle, and run-index HTML renderers read JSON packets and reject wrong schema versions where applicable
HTML write helpers render next to the JSON input instead of replacing the packet
artifact links rewrite JSON or spec markdown references to adjacent HTML views while retaining source labels
run-index discovery recognizes known packet files and flags whether an HTML projection exists
claim status Markdown is a human-readable projection over the selected claim packet, status summary, review results, validation reports, eval plans, refresh plans, and canonical map
the status report tells readers to use JSON packets as the audit source
browser review renders the Markdown report for human decisions without mutating the source report
browser comments are saved as a separate JSON packet with a report fingerprint
stale saved comments are ignored when the report fingerprint changes

decision.reason: The projection matrix defines the shipped Cautilus-owned readable artifact family scope, and focused Go and Node tests verify that listed views are generated from declared packet/source artifacts with JSON packets or source artifacts preserved as the audit source.
